patch:
diff --git a/dataclaw/anonymizer.py b/dataclaw/anonymizer.py
index 52c0aec..8e0638f 100644
--- a/dataclaw/anonymizer.py
+++ b/dataclaw/anonymizer.py
@@ -1,20 +1,6 @@
-"""Anonymize PII in Claude Code log data.
-
-NOTE: This is the buggy intermediate state for the `windows` branch in the
-Harbor task. It unifies ``anonymize_path`` into ``anonymize_text`` and adds
-partial Windows support, but intentionally contains the bugs the agent is
-expected to surface and fix:
-
-  * ``\\b`` word boundaries — fails when the username is adjacent to an
-    underscore (``config_alice_settings``).
-  * ``re.sub`` is called with a fresh pattern on every call — no caching.
-  * ``_replace_username`` does a plain ``str.replace`` — matches substrings,
-    so ``alex`` also replaces inside ``alexis``.
-  * No ``\\Users\\`` backslash support for short (<4 char) usernames.
-  * ``home`` parameter is accepted but never used for custom home dirs.
-  * No case-insensitive matching for short-username ``Users``/``home`` paths.
-"""
+"""Anonymize PII in Claude Code log data."""
 
+import functools
 import hashlib
 import os
 import re
@@ -30,33 +16,55 @@ def _detect_home_dir() -> tuple[str, str]:
     return home, username
 
 
-def anonymize_text(
-    text: str,
-    username: str,
-    username_hash: str,
-    home: str | None = None,
-) -> str:
-    if not text or not username:
-        return text
+@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=32)
+def _get_username_pattern(username: str) -> re.Pattern:
+    escaped = re.escape(username)
+    return re.compile(rf"(?<![a-zA-Z0-9]){escaped}(?![a-zA-Z0-9])", flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+
 
+@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=32)
+def _get_short_username_path_pattern(username: str) -> re.Pattern:
     escaped = re.escape(username)
+    return re.compile(rf"([/\\-]+(Users|home)[/\\-]+){escaped}(?=[^a-zA-Z0-9]|$)", flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+
+
+@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=32)
+def _get_custom_home_pattern(home: str) -> re.Pattern | None:
+    if home and not home.startswith(("/Users/", "/home/", "C:\\Users\\")):
+        home_escaped = home.replace("\\", "/")
+        home_escaped = re.escape(home_escaped)
+        home_escaped = home_escaped.replace("/", r"[/\\-]+")
+        home_escaped = home_escaped.replace(":", ":?")
+        return re.compile(home_escaped, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+    return None
+
+
+def anonymize_text(text: str, username: str, username_hash: str, home: str | None = None) -> str:
+    if not text or not username:
+        return text
 
     if len(username) >= 4:
-        # BUG: \b treats '_' as word char, so '_alice_' won't match.
-        return re.sub(rf"\b{escaped}\b", username_hash, text)
-
-    # Short username: handle POSIX-style /Users/<u> and /home/<u> paths only.
-    # BUG: no \Users\ (Windows) support for short usernames; not case-insensitive.
-    text = re.sub(
-        rf"([/\-]+(?:Users|home)[/\-]+){escaped}(?=[^a-zA-Z0-9]|$)",
-        rf"\g<1>{username_hash}",
-        text,
-    )
-    # BUG: home parameter is accepted but never applied.
+        pat = _get_username_pattern(username)
+        return pat.sub(username_hash, text)
+
+    # When username is < 3 chars, replace with more specific patterns
+    pat_path = _get_short_username_path_pattern(username)
+    text = pat_path.sub(rf"\g<1>{username_hash}", text)
+
+    if home:
+        pat_home = _get_custom_home_pattern(home)
+        if pat_home:
+            pat_user = _get_username_pattern(username)
+            def f(match):
+                # match.group(0) is a non-escaped string
+                return pat_user.sub(username_hash, match.group(0))
+
+            text = pat_home.sub(f, text)
+
     return text
 
 
-# Backward compatibility — windows branch unifies path handling into text.
+# Backward compatibility
 anonymize_path = anonymize_text
 
 
@@ -67,24 +75,37 @@ class Anonymizer:
         self.home, self.username = _detect_home_dir()
         self.username_hash = _hash_username(self.username)
 
+        # Additional usernames to anonymize (GitHub handles, Discord names, etc.)
         self._extra: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
-        for name in extra_usernames or []:
+        self._extra_dict: dict[str, str] = {}
+        
+        for name in (extra_usernames or []):
             name = name.strip()
             if name and name != self.username and len(name) >= 3:
-                self._extra.append((name, _hash_username(name)))
+                hashed = _hash_username(name)
+                self._extra.append((name, hashed))
+                self._extra_dict[name.lower()] = hashed
+                
+        if self._extra_dict:
+            escaped_names = [re.escape(k) for k in sorted(self._extra_dict.keys(), key=len, reverse=True)]
+            self._extra_pattern = re.compile(rf"(?<![a-zA-Z0-9])({'|'.join(escaped_names)})(?![a-zA-Z0-9])", flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+        else:
+            self._extra_pattern = None
 
     def path(self, file_path: str) -> str:
         return self.text(file_path)
 
     def text(self, content: str) -> str:
         result = anonymize_text(content, self.username, self.username_hash, self.home)
-        for name, hashed in self._extra:
-            result = _replace_username(result, name, hashed)
+        if self._extra_pattern:
+            def repl(match):
+                return self._extra_dict[match.group(1).lower()]
+            result = self._extra_pattern.sub(repl, result)
         return result
 
 
 def _replace_username(text: str, username: str, username_hash: str) -> str:
     if not text or not username or len(username) < 3:
         return text
-    # BUG: naive substring replace — replaces 'alex' inside 'alexis'.
-    return text.replace(username, username_hash)
+    pat = _get_username_pattern(username)
+    return pat.sub(username_hash, text)
_reconstruction_warnings: op[21] edit: edit input has no old/new text (export likely stripped content)
op[32] edit: edit input has no old/new text (export likely stripped content)
op[34] edit: edit input has no old/new text (export likely stripped content)
op[35] edit: edit input has no old/new text (export likely stripped content)
_verification_note: _fidelity = 'directional' driven by 4 'edit input has no old/new text' warnings (export-stripped intermediate Edits, not anchor mismatches). Curator stripped 8 noise files (bench_early_exit.py, benchmark.py, ad-hoc test_* scratchpads) leaving a single-file rewrite of dataclaw/anonymizer.py. The patch is self-consistent: introduces functools.lru_cache for regex pattern caching, restructures anonymize_text/anonymize_path (with anonymize_path = anonymize_text backcompat alias), adds _extra_dict/_extra_pattern batched-regex on Anonymizer. 13 mutating ops on 1 file with 57/-51 numstat is dense but coherent.

_review.note: directional fidelity (honest), schema complete, hdr=1/1 +57/-51 matches numstat, file aligned, 4 warnings

_patch_rebased_to_windows_branch.reason: patch failed to apply: dataclaw/anonymizer.py blob in windows-branch state (52c0aec) did not match patch index 045fe01
_patch_rebased_to_windows_branch.note: Regenerated 2026-05-17: original patch had base blob 045fe01 (main branch / upstream commit 3c0991467), but Harbor agent starts on the 'windows' branch where dataclaw/anonymizer.py is the synthesized buggy state (blob 52c0aec). Re-derived patch from windows-state -> final-state (blob 8e0638f unchanged). Old numstat 57/51, new 63/42.
